justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'omits the SPECIFIC operation' and 'omits the TARGET of the action'; (2) PII-in-logs risk identified with PCI-DSS and GDPR references; (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure; (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified; (5) Public internet exposure escalated to 'Coach K → Infrastructure / Security team' rather than unilaterally mandated; (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough audit log analysis — called out both missing operation type AND missing target entity, exactly matching the rubric criteria
PII-in-logs risk elevated beyond a generic flag — connected it to PCI-DSS Requirement 3 and GDPR Article 5, demonstrating compliance awareness
Stack trace leakage correctly framed as information disclosure with concrete details (class names, file paths, framework versions)
Public internet exposure properly escalated via routed_to fields to infrastructure/security teams rather than Pippen mandating network changes unilaterally
Went well beyond minimum rubric by identifying RBAC gap, health check stub, fail-open/fail-closed IDP concern, and missing rollback plan
The 3am on-call engineer framing in debugging_capability is an effective way to ground abstract gaps in operational reality
Correctly avoided the failure mode of mandating RBAC unilaterally — escalated it to 'Coach K → Identity / Security team' as an infrastructure gap requiring a team decision
Stub /health endpoint identified as masking cascading failures — noted it 'misleads load balancers and deployment tooling'
